name: Yamaha SeqTrack
use: Beat production, melodic step sequencing, DX FM synthesis, sampling, on-the-go sketching
overview: Compact battery-powered groovebox with 5 track types (Drums, Synth 1/2, DX Synth, Sampler), step sequencer per track, built-in AWM2+FM voices, scale/key lock, parameter locking, motion recording, and per-step probability. USB audio/MIDI, built-in speaker.
workflow: Select track (Drums / Synth 1 / Synth 2 / DX Synth / Sampler) → turn <kbd>SOUND</kbd> knob to pick preset
Set key: press <kbd>KEY</kbd> → choose root note C–B (including sharps)
Record drums: <kbd>HOLD</kbd> + <kbd>ALL</kbd> + KICK/SNARE/etc → realtime input mode → tap pads
Record synth steps: <kbd>HOLD</kbd> + <kbd>PAGE</kbd> + ↓ → step input mode → press step buttons to enter notes
Set pattern length (drums): <kbd>HOLD</kbd> + <kbd>PAGE</kbd> + turn encoder (press+turn for full bars)
Set pattern length (synth): <kbd>HOLD</kbd> + turn encoder; use PAGE to navigate bars
Parameter lock: hold a step button + turn <kbd>SOUND</kbd> knob → param locked for that step only ★
Motion recording: <kbd>HOLD</kbd> + REC button + turn any knob → automation recorded per step ★

shortcuts.action: Set scale: Minor, Major, Minor Pent, Major Pent, Lydian, Dorian, Mixolydian, Phrygian ★
Set root key: C C# D D# E F F# G G# A A# B ★
Pattern change quantization (each unit = 1 measure; applies to Scene changes too)
Drum pattern length (press+turn = full bars / 4-step increments) ★
Micro timing per step (−60 to +58 ticks) ★
Enable realtime input mode for drum part ★

shortcuts.combo: [HOLD] + step buttons → select notes with drum keys
shortcuts.action: Edit chord notes on a synth step ★
Change note length for all steps
Change recording source: MIC/IN / USB / Resample ★
Monitoring mode — adjust incoming audio level
Enter Song mode — cycles NORMAL → SONG → SCENE ★
Adjust scene length (far left = AUTO: length of longest pattern) ★
Loop scene; double-tap again to double-loop ★
Adjust PAN / VOL / REVERB / DELAY per track ★
Create parameter lock — per-step sound change ★
Motion recording — lights flash red if param can't be recorded ★
Amp Attack | Amp Decay | Cutoff | Resonance ★
Reverb Send | Delay Send | EQ High Gain | EQ Low Gain
Portamento Time | Arp Type | Arp Gate Time | Arp Speed
Start Point | End Point | Pitch Attack | Pitch Decay
FM Algorithm | Modulator Amount | Modulator Frequency | Modulator Feedback ★

tips: Temp Save / Restore (yellow/cyan LEDs): quick snapshot within a session — great for A/B comparing edits without overwriting the project
Pattern change quantization (PROJECT+ALL+encoder): sets how many measures before a pattern switch takes effect — prevents jarring mid-bar changes
Substep (HOLD + drum step): divides a single step into finer subdivisions — use for hi-hat rolls or ghost notes within one 16th note ★
Edit Chord (hold synth step → tap drum keys): change the individual notes of a chord stored in a synth step — polyphonic step editing
Scene mode vs Song mode: Song = scenes play in sequence (arrangement); Scene = scenes play as loops (live performance). Both accessible from PAUSE
Loop scene (double-tap): great for live performance — loop a section while preparing the next part, double-tap to extend the loop length
Probability values: 10 / 23 / 36 / 49 / 61 / 74 / 87 / 100% — set hi-hats to 61–74% for a humanised groove without programming every variation
Octave range: +2 to −3 — use −2/−3 for deep sub bass, +1/+2 for high arpeggios
Mixer mode (ALL+MUTE): adjust PAN/VOL/REVERB/DELAY per track using the 4 right encoders — fastest way to balance a mix
DX Synth FM params (page 5) only after Advanced Sound Editing (hold SOUND 2s) — FM Algorithm changes operator routing topology
